message.content:
Explore the Universe feature's component structure and rendering pipeline. I need to understand:

1. All files in `client/src/components/Universe/` - list them all and understand the directory structure
2. The `UniverseMinimap` component - how it renders
3. The `useUniverseCamera` hook - how camera/viewport works, what `flyTo`, `jumpTo`, `getLayerProps` do
4. The CSS module at `client/src/components/Universe/Universe.module.css` - what styles exist, especially for UI panels, buttons, overlays
5. How the `Stage` (react-konva) is structured with layers - the layer ordering and what each layer renders
6. The `DeepSpaceLayer`, `MidGalaxyLayer`, `NebulaLayer` components - brief understanding of what they render
7. Check for any existing toggle buttons or mode buttons in the Universe UI
8. Look at how `selectedPost` and `selectedGalaxy` panels work in Universe.jsx - the preview panel pattern

Be thorough in listing all files and understanding the component hierarchy.

content.text: I'll explore the Universe feature's component structure and rendering pipeline thoroughly. Let me start by examining the directory structure and then dive into each component.
Perfect! Now let me read all the key files to understand the complete structure. I'll start with the main Universe component and the key files.
Now let me read the layer components and other important files:
Now let me read the WormholeLayer and a few utility files to complete the picture:

Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information about the Universe feature. Let me create a detailed report: ## Universe Feature Component Structure & Rendering Pipeline ### 1. Directory Structure All files located in: `/client/src/components/Universe/` ``` Universe/ ├── Universe.jsx (Main component, ~637 lines) ├── Universe.module.css (Styling module, ~483 lines) │ ├── hooks/ │ ├── useUniverseCamera.js (Camera & viewport management) │ └── useCosmicGravity.js (Galaxy physics simulation) │ ├── layers/ │ ├── DeepSpaceLayer.jsx (Background nebulae & dust stars) │ ├── MidGalaxyLayer.jsx (Mid-range colored stars) │ ├── NebulaLayer.jsx (Galaxy nebulae with breathing animation) │ ├── InteractiveLayer.jsx (Stars, galaxies, edges, labels) │ └── WormholeLayer.jsx (Wormhole vortex connections) │ ├── components/ │ ├── UniverseMinimap.jsx (Canvas-based navigation minimap) │ ├── StarGroup.jsx (Individual star rendering with glows) │ └── Nebula.jsx (Procedural nebula cloudlets) │ └── utils/ ├── starUtils.js (Color, radius, hash functions) ├── cosmicPhysics.js (Force calculations, edges) ├── proceduralGeneration.js (Star generation) ├── galaxyVisuals.js (Galaxy spiral rendering) ├── embeddingToColor.js (Embedding → color conversion) ├── coordConversion.js (Coordinate transformations) └── starUtils.js (Utility functions) ``` --- ### 2. useUniverseCamera Hook **Location:** `/hooks/useUniverseCamera.js` **Key State:** - `camera`: { x, y } — world-space center position - `zoom`: scalar from `MIN_UNIVERSE_ZOOM` to `MAX_UNIVERSE_ZOOM` - `isDragging`: boolean flag for cursor state - `stageSize`: { width, height } **Primary Methods:** | Method | Purpose | Behavior | |--------|---------|----------| | **getLayerProps(parallaxFactor)** | Transforms layer for parallax scrolling | Returns `{ x, y, scaleX, scaleY }` based on camera, zoom, and parallax factor (0.1 = far back, 1.0 = interactive layer) | | **flyTo(targetX, targetY, targetZoom, onComplete)** | Cinematic camera movement | Zoom out → pan → zoom in (parabolic arc); 1800ms fixed duration; distance-based zoom-out depth | | **jumpTo(targetX, targetY)** | Instant camera teleport | Cancels any in-progress flights; useful for minimap drag mode | | **getViewportBounds()** | Calculates visible world region | Returns `{ minX, maxX, minY, maxY }` with padding; used for data fetching bounds | **Input Handling:** - Mouse drag: pans camera based on pointer delta and zoom - Wheel: zoom with cursor-locked pan (world point stays under cursor) - Touch single finger: behaves like mouse drag - Touch pinch: scales zoom while keeping center of two fingers stationary - Stage events all registered in `stageEvents` object **Flight Animation:** - Uses `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth animation - Cubic ease-in-out for panning timing - Parabolic arc for zoom (quadratic interpolation between start, mid, target) - Travel distance determines how far to zoom out during flight --- ### 3. CSS Module (Universe.module.css) **Location:** `/Universe.module.css` (~483 lines) **Key Classes & Elements:** | Class | Purpose | Styling | |-------|---------|---------| | `.universeContainer` | Full-screen stage wrapper | Fixed positioning, dark radial gradient bg, `touch-action: none`, `cursor: grab` | | `.universeContainer.grabbing` | Active drag state | `cursor: grabbing` | | `.backButton` | Top-left nav button | Glass morphism, rgba bg (0.06), border-radius 12px, hover effects | | `.distanceTracker` | Bottom-left distance display | Monospace font, very subtle text, pointer-events: none | | `.loadingOverlay` | Full-screen loading state | Centered spinner card with glass morphism, z-index 1040 | | `.loadingSpinner` | Animated spinner | 26px, rotating border, blue top color | | `.previewPanel` | Bottom-right post preview | 320px width, glass morphism, z-index 1010, author avatar, title, snippet, likes, action buttons | | `.previewReadBtn` / `.previewClose` | Post preview controls | Blue theme (5f92ff), hover transitions | | `.wormholeBtn` / `.wormholeTooltip` | Wormhole travel button | Special hover tooltip, positioned absolute | | `.galaxyPreviewPanel` | Bottom-right galaxy profile | Similar to preview panel, includes background image, centered avatar overlay, bio, visit button | | `.minimap` | Bottom-left navigation | 160px square, canvas element, crosshair cursor, z-index 1010 | | `.warpOverlay` | Wormhole travel FX | Full-screen, radial gradient animation (warpPulse), 1.8s opacity pulse | **Glass Morphism Style:** - `background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06)` (very subtle white) - `backdrop-filter: blur(16px)` with `-webkit-` prefix - `border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1)` (subtle outline) - `border-radius: 16px` (12-16px typical) **Color Scheme:** - Primary accent: `#5f92ff` (blue) - Text: `#e0e0e0` / `rgba(255,255,255,0.55)` (varying opacity) - Background: `radial-gradient(ellipse at center, #0a0e1a 0%, #000000 100%)` **Responsive Design:** - Media query for `max-width: 600px` - Mobile: distanceTracker moves to top-right, panels adjust width, minimap shrinks to 100px --- ### 4. Stage (react-konva) Layer Structure **Location:** `Universe.jsx` lines 502-540 **Layer Ordering (Back to Front):** ```jsx 1. DeepSpaceLayer (parallaxFactor: 0.1) — Background nebulae & dust 2. MidGalaxyLayer (parallaxFactor: 0.4) — Mid-range colored stars 3. NebulaLayer (parallaxFactor: 0.85) — Galaxy nebulae clouds + stardust 4. WormholeLayer (parallaxFactor: 1.0) — Wormhole vortices (conditional) 5. InteractiveLayer (parallaxFactor: 1.0) — Posts, galaxies, bridges, labels ``` **Parallax Effect:** - `getLayerProps(factor)` applies: `x: halfScreenW - cameraX * factor * zoom` - Lower factor = stays more fixed (parallax background effect) - Factor 1.0 = moves with camera (interactive elements) **Stage Props:** - `ref={stageRef}` — ref to Konva Stage for pointer position, etc. - `width={stageSize.width}`, `height={stageSize.height}` — responsive - `{...stageEvents}` — mouse/touch/wheel handlers from camera hook --- ### 5. Layer Components Overview #### **DeepSpaceLayer.jsx** - **Purpose:** Far background parallax layer - **Contents:** - 18 procedural nebulae (large, semi-transparent clouds) - 2000 dust stars (small, scattered points) - **Rendering:** Single `Shape` with `sceneFunc` for batch drawing - **Performance:** `listening={false}` (no interaction), `perfectDrawEnabled={false}` #### **MidGalaxyLayer.jsx** - **Purpose:** Mid-range depth layer (parallax 0.4) - **Contents:** - 800 mid-galaxy stars with radial gradient glows - Pre-parsed RGB colors for sceneFunc efficiency - **Rendering:** Single Shape with gradient circles - **Color:** Hex colors converted to RGB, drawn with radial gradient (center bright → edges fade) #### **NebulaLayer.jsx** - **Purpose:** Breathing nebula clouds around galaxies + stardust particles - **Key Animation Parameters:** - `BREATH_PERIOD`: 10000ms (10s breathing cycle) - `ROTATION_SPEED`: 0.002 radians per frame (slow rotation) - Stardust: individual twinkling with drift motion - **Contents Per Galaxy:** - Rotating nebula cloud (BASE_RADIUS + 50px per post, max 4 posts) - 60 stardust particles with individual animations - **Stardust Animation:** - `twinkleSpeed`: 0.0008-0.0028 per frame (opacity sine wave) - `driftSpeed`: 0.00003-0.00008 per frame (orbital drift) - `driftRadius`: 3-15px orbital offset - Base alpha: 0.08-0.43, size: 0.4-2.0px - **Rendering:** Single Shape, `requestAnimationFrame` loop with `batchDraw()` #### **InteractiveLayer.jsx** - **Purpose:** All interactive elements and connections - **Components:** 1. **Semantic Bridges** (Shape) — curved lines between similar posts - Max 120 pairs per frame - Filter by similarity > 0.9 - Quadratic curve with shadow glow 2. **Galaxy Visuals** (Shape) — spiral galaxy spirals at each galaxy position - Drawn via `drawGalaxyVisuals()` helper - Connection lines between galaxies (edges) 3. **Galaxy Labels** (Group + Text) — clickable names - Scale inverted by zoom to stay readable - Hover color change, pointer cursor feedback 4. **Stars** (StarGroup components, viewport-culled) - Rendered only if within visible bounds + buffer - Each is a Group with glow circles and title text #### **WormholeLayer.jsx** - **Purpose:** Semantic wormhole connections between distant posts - **Key Features:** - Each wormhole has two vortex endpoints - Vortex rendering (per endpoint): - Large pulsing halo (outer glow) - Inner bright glow - Dark void center - Bright event horizon ring - 4 concentric rotating rings with gaps - 3 spiral arms with particles spiraling inward - Outer orbiting particles (larger, slower) - Bright white core - Connection line: dashed line with glow, animated dash offset - **Animation:** - Breathing (sine wave based on time) - Ring rotation (speeds vary by ring, alternating direction) - Spiral arms spiral inward - Outer particles orbit - Opacity scales with zoom (brighter when zoomed out) --- ### 6. selectedPost & selectedGalaxy Preview Panels **Location:** `Universe.jsx` lines 586-631 (post), 552-583 (galaxy) **Post Preview Panel Pattern:** ```jsx {selectedPost && (
{hasWormhole && }
)} ``` **Triggers:** - `selectedPost` set by `handleStarClick(post)` (line 317) - `handleReadPost()` navigates to `/home/post/{postId}` - `handleWormholeTravel()` flies to wormhole target post **Galaxy Preview Panel Pattern:** ```jsx {selectedGalaxy && galaxyProfile && (
)} ``` **Triggers:** - `selectedGalaxy` set by `handleGalaxyClick(galaxy)` (line 332) - Fetches full profile via `getUserData(userId)` API - `handleClickProfile()` navigates to user's profile --- ### 7. Existing Toggle/Mode Buttons **No dedicated toggle buttons currently exist** in the Universe UI. The interface is relatively minimal: - **Back Button** (top-left) — only navigation button - **Preview Close Buttons** (top-right of panels) — dismiss panels - **Read Button** (in post preview) — navigate to post - **Visit Profile Button** (in galaxy preview) — navigate to profile - **Wormhole Button** (conditional in post preview) — travel to connected post **No mode toggles for:** layer visibility, view types, semantic bridge display, gravity simulation visualization, or other features. --- ### 8. Summary Table: Component Hierarchy | Component | Renders | State | Key Props | |-----------|---------|-------|-----------| | **Universe** | Main stage + overlays | posts, selectedPost, selectedGalaxy, galaxies, wormholes | — | | **useUniverseCamera** | — | camera, zoom, isDragging | userId, hasOwnPosts | | **useCosmicGravity** | — | galaxyPositions, isSettled | galaxies, edges, posts | | **DeepSpaceLayer** | Nebulae + dust | — | layerProps | | **MidGalaxyLayer** | Colored stars | — | layerProps | | **NebulaLayer** | Galaxy clouds + stardust | animRef (phase, rotations) | layerProps, galaxies, centroids | | **WormholeLayer** | Vortices + connections | animRef (time) | layerProps, wormholes, zoom | | **InteractiveLayer** | Stars, galaxies, bridges | — | layerProps, posts, galaxies, zoom, ... | | **StarGroup** | Star circle + glow + title | groupRef, tweenRef | x, y, radius, color, isPulsing, onClick | | **GalaxyLabel** | Clickable text label | — | x, y, name, zoom, onClick | | **UniverseMinimap** | Canvas minimap | canvasRef, pointerStateRef | galaxies, camera, zoom, onNavigate | --- This comprehensive exploration reveals a sophisticated, layered rendering system with parallax scrolling, procedural animation, interactive elements, and semantic visualization of relationships. The Universe is a fully immersive, interactive space with smooth camera mechanics and responsive UI panels.
